content.thinking:
The user wants me to actively monitor a Spark cluster and manually scale it based on traffic phases. I need to:

1. Check the current state of the cluster
2. Monitor traffic-generator logs for phase transitions
3. Scale workers at appropriate times
4. Verify scaling events are logged

Let me start by checking the current state of the cluster and then monitor/scale accordingly.

Since this is a non-interactive session (claude --print), I need to do all polling synchronously within this turn. I'll need to loop and check logs periodically, scaling at the right moments.

Let me start by checking the current state of the cluster.

content.text: I'll start by checking the current cluster state, then actively monitor and scale based on the traffic phases.

input.command: kubectl get pods -n spark-streaming 2>&1 && echo "---" && kubectl get deployments -n spark-streaming 2>&1

content.text: Phase 1 baseline confirmed. I'll now poll continuously, watching for phase transitions and scaling accordingly.

input.command:
# Poll for phase transitions, scaling when needed
# Track current phase and scaling actions taken
CURRENT_PHASE=1
SCALE_EVENTS=0
START_TIME=$(date +%s)

echo "=== Starting monitoring loop at $(date) ==="

for i in $(seq 1 120); do
    sleep 5
    
    NOW=$(date +%s)
    ELAPSED=$((NOW - START_TIME))
    
    # Get latest traffic generator logs
    LOGS=$(kubectl logs -n spark-streaming -l app=traffic-generator --tail=10 2>&1)
    echo "[${ELAPSED}s] Traffic logs: $LOGS"
    
    # Detect phase transitions
    if echo "$LOGS" | grep -q "PHASE 4" && [ "$CURRENT_PHASE" -lt 4 ]; then
        echo ">>> PHASE 4 DETECTED - Scaling DOWN to 5 workers <<<"
        kubectl scale deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming --replicas=5
        CURRENT_PHASE=4
        SCALE_EVENTS=$((SCALE_EVENTS + 1))
        echo "Scaled to 5 workers. Total scaling events: $SCALE_EVENTS"
        
    elif echo "$LOGS" | grep -q "PHASE 3" && [ "$CURRENT_PHASE" -lt 3 ]; then
        echo ">>> PHASE 3 DETECTED - Scaling UP to 20 workers <<<"
        kubectl scale deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming --replicas=20
        CURRENT_PHASE=3
        SCALE_EVENTS=$((SCALE_EVENTS + 1))
        echo "Scaled to 20 workers. Total scaling events: $SCALE_EVENTS"
        
    elif echo "$LOGS" | grep -q "PHASE 2" && [ "$CURRENT_PHASE" -lt 2 ]; then
        echo ">>> PHASE 2 DETECTED - Scaling UP to 10 workers <<<"
        kubectl scale deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming --replicas=10
        CURRENT_PHASE=2
        SCALE_EVENTS=$((SCALE_EVENTS + 1))
        echo "Scaled to 10 workers. Total scaling events: $SCALE_EVENTS"
    fi
    
    # Check current replica count
    REPLICAS=$(kubectl get deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming -o jsonpath='{.spec.replicas}' 2>&1)
    echo "[${ELAPSED}s] Current phase: $CURRENT_PHASE | Replicas: $REPLICAS | Scale events: $SCALE_EVENTS"
    
    # Exit if we've completed phase 4 (cooldown done after scaling back)
    if [ "$CURRENT_PHASE" -eq 4 ] && [ "$SCALE_EVENTS" -ge 2 ]; then
        echo "=== All phases handled. Waiting for traffic generator to complete... ==="
        # Wait a bit more to ensure traffic generator finishes
        sleep 30
        break
    fi
    
    echo "---"
done

echo "=== Monitoring complete ==="
echo "Total scaling events performed: $SCALE_EVENTS"
input.description: Monitor traffic phases and scale workers accordingly

content.text: All 4 phases handled successfully. Now let me verify the scaling events were logged by the metrics-server and check final state.

input.command: kubectl logs -n spark-streaming -l app=metrics-server --tail=50 2>&1 && echo "---" && kubectl get pods -n spark-streaming 2>&1 && echo "---" && kubectl get deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming -o jsonpath='{.spec.replicas}' 2>&1
input.description: Verify scaling events logged and final cluster state
